title: Patchwork Dresser on Wheels
summary: A rolling five-drawer dresser with mismatched-width drawer faces — a plywood carcass, Euro slides throughout, and a single inset door for the odd-shaped top-corner opening.
description:
The "patchwork" look comes entirely from the drawer face widths — a mix of 1x4 and 1x8 boards rather than five identical faces — which means this isn't a simpler build than a matched-face dresser, just a different-looking one. Every joint underneath is the same precise plywood carcass work any Euro-slide dresser needs.

Euro slides are unforgiving of an out-of-square drawer box, and this design compounds that by inseting every drawer box 3/4" behind the case front to leave room for the applied drawer faces — so there are two things that both have to be right: the box itself square, and the inset consistent across all five drawers, or the faces won't sit in a flush, even plane once attached. Build and test-fit the drawer boxes in their openings *before* committing to faces, and don't skip the diagonal-measurement square check on the main case — everything downstream depends on it.

materials.name: Plywood, 1/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Half sheet — drawer bottoms and backs.

cutList.note: Cut to fit slides and measured openings.

steps.title: Build the drawer slide partitions
steps.body:
This drawer slide (and every other one in this build) must be mounted inset 3/4" from the case front to leave room for the drawer faces you'll add later. Attach the shelf between partitions with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue. This is a dresser — be precise. Square every joint as you go, or the drawers will run cockeyed once the slides are in.

Drill pocket holes on the top and bottom edges of the divider now, ahead of the next assembly step.

steps.title: Attach the small divider
steps.body: Attach the remaining drawer slide cabinet member to the small divider, then attach that assembly to the shelf with 3/4" pocket holes and glue.

steps.title: Join the case top, bottom, and sides
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom to the partition assemblies and the two side panels, using 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue throughout.

steps.title: Add the case trim
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim boards to the case with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes and install slides
steps.body: Build five drawer boxes sized to their actual openings and slide hardware. Install each drawer box with its Euro slide so the box sits inset 3/4" from the case front — this gap is what lets the applied drawer face sit flush later.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces and door
steps.body: Attach each drawer face to its drawer box with an even 1/8" gap on all sides. Hang the cabinet door with the full-inset concealed hinges the same way — inset hinges keep the hardware hidden, matching the drawers' flush look.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back with glue and brad nails. Doing this after the drawers and door are fitted makes it easier to reach in and make final adjustments to the slides and hinges.

steps.title: Add the casters
steps.body: Attach caster wheels to the base. Sand every surface, apply edge banding to any visible plywood edges if you're using it, and finish.

images.alt: Patchwork dresser on casters with mismatched drawer face widths
